title: Associate
description:
Who we are

We are one of the largest international law firms in the world. With over 30 offices across the globe, we strive to exceed the expectations of our clients, providing them with the highest-quality advice and legal insight, which combines the firm’s global standards with in-depth local expertise.

Our firm, work and people span jurisdictions, cultures, and languages. We offer our clients a truly international perspective. We believe every career should be rewarding and stimulating - full of opportunities to learn, thrive, and grow. That’s why we’re so proud of our inclusive, friendly, and team-based approach to work.

You’ll find our clients in commercial and industrial sectors, the financial investor community, governments, regulators, trade bodies, and not-for-profit organisations. But no matter who they are or why they’ve reached out to us, we provide a world-class service every step of the way. And that’s possible thanks to the entrepreneurial spirit and conscientious approach to work that you’ll find across all of our teams.

Whichever area of the business you join, you’ll become an integral part an innovative, diverse and ambitious team of people. Clifford Chance is a place where the brightest minds and the best of colleagues meet

The role

We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Capital Markets Associate to join our dynamic team in Singapore. The successful candidate will be involved in a wide range of DCM, ECM transactions, including but not limited to initial public offerings, equity placements, bond issuances, and debt restructuring. This role requires a good understanding of financial markets, as well as the ability to navigate complex legal and regulatory frameworks across multiple jurisdictions.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Draft and review legal documentation for capital markets transactions, including prospectuses, offering circulars, subscription agreements, and trust deeds.
  • Advise clients on the structuring, establishment, and operation of debt issuance programmes and equity offerings.
  • Conduct legal research and provide guidance on regulatory compliance, corporate governance, and market practices.
  • Liaise with regulatory authorities, stock exchanges, trustees, and other stakeholders throughout the transaction lifecycle.
  • Participate in client meetings and calls to discuss transaction structures and legal issues.
  • Collaborate with cross-border teams to ensure seamless execution of multi-jurisdictional deals.
  • Monitor market developments and contribute to thought leadership and client updates.
  • Engage in internal knowledge-sharing initiatives and contribute to practice development.

Qualifications

Your experience

  • Qualified lawyer with a minimum of 1 - 3 years PQE in capital markets within a reputable law firm
  • Strong academic credentials in law.
  • Excellent legal drafting and negotiation skills.
  • Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and interpersonal skills.
  • A team player with a commitment to delivering high-quality work.

postedCompany.description:
Clifford Chance is one of the UK's 'Magic Circle' law firms with an integrated global team across 35 offices in 25 countries and more than 3,300 legal advisers. We operate across Asia Pacific with offices in Beijing, Hong Kong, Perth, Shanghai, Singapore, Sydney and Tokyo. With over 400 lawyers in Asia alone, it is one of the largest international firms in the region, enjoying a market leading reputation across a number of the practices.

At Clifford Chance, you can expect to work with high-calibre professionals and be challenged every day. We expect a high level of performance and offer benefits to match which, along with our great offices in Marina Bay Financial Centre and friendly team, make this a great place to work.
postedCompany.name: CLIFFORD CHANCE PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
